title: brachypodium unioloides
intro: Brachypodium unioloides, commonly known as the button grass or button grass sedge, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand, and is often found in wetland areas. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Brachypodium unioloides include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
